title: Tenant Profile v1
description: Canonical capability and safety profile for a tenant in the policy/blast kernel.

tenantId.description: Stable tenant identifier used across SQLite, Dead-Ledger, and MCP.

kernelBacking.description: Facts about how much of the policy/blast model is backed by real host kernels.

iptablesSnapshotMode.description: How iptables state is modeled for this tenant.

supportsLiveHashDriftCheck.description: True if iptables hash drift is monitored and exposed to MCP tools.

lastSnapshotAt.description: Timestamp of most recent known-good iptables snapshot, if any.

snapshotStalenessSec.description: Maximum allowed age in seconds before a snapshot is considered stale.

blastCapabilities.description: What the tenant is allowed to do with blast_radius/propose tools.

allowBlastRadiusQueries.description: If false, blast_radius MCP tool must always fail closed for this tenant.

allowPropose.description: If false, propose MCP tool must always reject new policy changes.

maxBlastTier.description: Highest blast tier this tenant can request (T0–T3).

maxNetChangeCorridorTier.description: Highest governance corridor tier allowed for net-change evaluations.

requireKernelBackedForPropose.description: If true, propose must fail when iptablesSnapshotMode != continuous-sync.

requireKernelBackedForBlastRadius.description: If true, blast_radius must fail when iptablesSnapshotMode == none.

governance.description: Pointers into corridors, drift thresholds, and admission rules.

policyDriftMax.description: Maximum tolerated policy drift D before new proposals must be rejected.

denyOnDrift.description: If true, MCP tools must reject propose when drift > policyDriftMax.

mcpToolPolicy.description: Fine-grained flags for individual MCP tools.
